mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. The mechanism is always active and does not require a regime threshold.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of rolling windows when INDPRO is above its 6-month moving average, or if the rank IC over the same horizon is below 0.03 across any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, it signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates, depresses equity valuations, and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is only active above this threshold.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or if the average return difference between tightening and non-tightening periods is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, it indicates a very tight labor market that leads to wage inflation and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The trigger is a low-unemployment threshold.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE < 4.5%, or if the cumulative return difference between below-threshold and above-threshold periods is not negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of a low federal funds rate (below 3%) and accelerating consumer price inflation (CPI YoY > 2%) creates negative real interest rates, which incentivizes equity investment as an inflation hedge and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when both conditions hold, or if the joint signal does not produce a significantly higher mean return than individual signals alone (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-market overheating regime (HOUST persistently above 2000 and rising month-over-month), further increases in housing starts signal speculative overbuilding, which foreshadows a housing correction and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. In a normal regime, rising HOUST would be positive.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST > 2000 and rising, or if the negative return condition does not hold in other regimes (i.e., direction should be positive when HOUST < 2000).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflect strong consumer demand and income growth, which directly boost corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-trading-day horizon. The signal operates continuously without any threshold activation.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of rolling windows when RSAFS is above its 3-month moving average, or if the rank IC over 20-day horizon is below 0.03 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) increases by more than 200,000 in a month, it signals above-trend job creation that boosts investor confidence and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days. The trigger is a high-nonfarm-payroll-change threshold.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when the monthly payroll change exceeds 200,000, or if the average return on trigger days is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously show robust monthly growth (INDPRO > 0.5% MoM and RSAFS > 1% MoM), it confirms that the expansion is broad-based across manufacturing and consumption, which strongly supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. The signal requires both conditions to be met.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth > 0.5% and RSAFS monthly growth > 1%
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when both growth thresholds are met, or if the joint signal does not outperform either single-series signal in terms of mean return over any 3-year walk-forward.
